question: Can you find out the Manhattan neighborhood containing a top 7 high school that appeared in the top 7 by overall score for 3 consecutive school years starting from the year from 2005-2010 that has the lowest average Mega Ball. Then, find out which NYC structure was built with what this neighborhood is named after and was designed by Stanford White. Report in what year is this structure designed.

1.fact: Averaging Mega Ball by draw year for 2005-2010, 2007 has the lowest average at 22.155339805825243.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2005-2010 has the lowest average Mega Ball? (Parse Draw Date to Year; compute the mean Mega Ball by Year; select the minimum.)

2.fact: In 2007-08, the top 7 NYC high schools by overall score were: Brooklyn International HS (106.5), Gregorio Luperon HS (100.4), HS for Contemporary Arts (99.1), Bedford Academy HS (98.7), Manhattan Bridges HS (97.8), Marble Hill HS for International Studies (97.5), New World HS (97.0).
2.subquestion: Which NYC high schools were in the top 7 by overall score in the school year starting from the year identified in node 1 (2007)?
answer: Marble Hill High School for International Studies

3.fact: In 2008-09, the top 7 NYC high schools by overall score were: HS of Hospitality Management (105.3), Bedford Academy HS (103.3), Williamsburg Prep (102.3), South Bronx Prep (101.7), Marble Hill HS (101.3), HS for Violin and Dance (98.9), Essex Street Academy (95.1).
3.subquestion: Which NYC high schools were in the top 7 by overall score in 2008-09?
answer: Marble Hill High School for International Studies
High School for Violin and Dance

4.fact: In 2009-10, the top 7 NYC high schools by overall score were: Theatre Arts Production Company School (106.3), Brooklyn International HS (103.0), Williamsburg Prep (101.7), Marble Hill HS (100.5), Williamsburg Architecture (100.2), Manhattan Village Academy (96.6), HS for Violin and Dance (95.8).
4.subquestion: Which NYC high schools were in the top 7 by overall score in 2009-10?
answer: Marble Hill High School for International Studies
Williamsburg High School for Architecture and Design
High School for Violin and Dance

5.fact: The Marble Hill neighborhood gained its current name in 1891 from the Tuckahoe marble deposits discovered underneath it. Marble Hill High School for International Studies is located in this neighborhood.
5.subquestion: What is the neighborhood containing <intersection of node_1, node_2, node_3> named after?

6.fact: NYC structures built with Tuckahoe marble include the Washington Square Arch (1891), Federal Hall National Memorial (1842), Brooklyn Borough Hall (1849), St. Patrick's Cathedral (1858-78), Tweed Courthouse (1861-72), Grace Episcopal Church (1846), and Marble Collegiate Church (1854).
6.subquestion: Which NYC structures were built with <node_5 answer>?

7.fact: Stanford White designed several NYC structures including the Washington Square Arch (1891), the second Madison Square Garden (1890, demolished), the Cable Building (1893), the Villard Houses (1884), the Gould Memorial Library (1900), and Judson Memorial Church (1892).
7.subquestion: Which NYC structures were designed by Stanford White?

8.fact: Designed by architect Stanford White in 1891, it commemorates the centennial of George Washington's 1789 inauguration as President of the United States, and forms the southern terminus of Fifth Avenue.
8.subquestion: In what year is <intersection of node_5, node_6> designed?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Year with lowest average Mega Ball (2005-2010) -> 2007
Node 2: Top 7 NYC high schools by overall score in 2007-08
Node 3: Top 7 NYC high schools by overall score in 2008-09
Node 4: Top 7 NYC high schools by overall score in 2009-10
Intersection: Marble Hill High School for International Studies
Node 5: Marble Hill neighborhood named after Tuckahoe marble
Node 6: NYC structures built with Tuckahoe marble
Node 7: NYC structures designed by Stanford White
